records.ety: [OE. bulden, bilden, AS. byldan to build, fr. bold house; cf. Icel. bōl farm, abode, Dan. bol small farm, OSw. bol, böle, house, dwelling, fr. root of Icel. būa to dwell; akin to E. be, bower, boor. √97.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Built (blt); p. pr. & vb. n. Building. The regular imp. & p. p. Builded is antiquated.]

defs: To erect or construct, as an edifice or fabric of any kind; to form by uniting materials into a regular structure; to fabricate; to make; to raise.
quotes: Nor aught availed him now To have built in heaven high towers.

defs: To raise or place on a foundation; to form, establish, or produce by using appropriate means.
quotes: Who builds his hopes in air of your good looks.

defs: To increase and strengthen; to increase the power and stability of; to settle, or establish, and preserve; -- frequently with up; as, to build up one's constitution.
quotes: I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up.

defs: To exercise the art, or practice the business, of building.
To rest or depend, as on a foundation; to ground one's self or one's hopes or opinions upon something deemed reliable; to rely; as, to build on the opinions or advice of others.
Form or mode of construction; general figure; make; as, the build of a ship; a great build on a man.
